notes: SUB-03 fatigue engine golden cases: FMCSA Hours-of-Service (property-carrying CMV, 49 CFR 395.3) + EU Regulation (EC) 561/2006 window-math compliance flags, plus the advisory fatigue index.
Compliance flags are EXACT booleans derived from the regulations (authoritative output). A True flag = compliant with that rule; False = breach.

FMCSA limits (LOCKED): 11 h driving / 14 h on-duty window / 30-min break after 8 cumulative driving h / 60-h-in-7-days or 70-h-in-8-days cycle / 34 h restart. Source: fmcsa.dot.gov Summary of HOS Regulations.

EU 561/2006 limits (LOCKED): 9 h daily driving (10 h twice/week) / 45-min break after 4.5 h driving / 11 h daily rest (9 h up to 3x) / 56 h weekly / 90 h fortnightly. Source: transport.ec.europa.eu, eur-lex.europa.eu.

The fatigue_index is ADVISORY / derived (weighted-stressor metric, owner-confirmed), NOT a regulatory threshold. Determinism tolerance: same input -> byte-identical output, scores equal within +/-0.001.

Duty log input shape: a list of duty-status segments, each {status, hours}, where status is one of driving | on_duty | off_duty | sleeper. Hours are floats. extensions_used (EU) = number of 10 h daily-driving extensions already used this week.
FAT-5 (invalid: negative hours / >24 h in a day -> FatigueInputError) is asserted in the test, NOT encoded as a fixture case (D-03).

cases.why: 12 h total driving in one shift exceeds the FMCSA 11 h driving limit -> driving_11h FAIL; the 12.5 h on-duty span is still within the 14 h window and a >=30-min break fell before 8 cumulative driving h.
5 h continuous driving with no break breaches EU 561's mandatory 45-min break after 4.5 h continuous driving -> break_45min_after_4.5h FAIL; 5 h total is still within the 9 h daily-driving limit.

9.5 h total driving exceeds the 9 h base daily limit but is within the 10 h twice-weekly extension; extensions_used=1 means a 2nd extension is still available -> daily_driving PASS. Each continuous driving stretch (4.5/4.0/1.0 h) stays <= the 4.5 h Art. 7 ceiling with a 45-min break between -> break_45min PASS. NOTE (Rule-1 deviation): the original fixture used a single 5.0 h post-break driving block, which regulatory-correctly BREACHES Art. 7 (a fresh 4.5 h limit applies after each break); the log was split so FAT-3 isolates the extension-aware daily_driving test as intended.

Same 9.5 h driving but extensions_used=2 means both twice-weekly 10 h extensions are already spent -> the 9 h base limit binds -> daily_driving FAIL. Proves the engine is extension-aware. Break rule still PASS (each continuous stretch <= 4.5 h).

A clean compliant shift: 8 h total driving (<=11 FMCSA, <=9 EU), a 45-min break after 4 h, 11 h daily rest, daytime (10:00, outside WOCL) -> all flags PASS and fatigue_index lands a deterministic value in [0,1] with a Low/Moderate band.
